a11y.guideline_desc: Consider assistive technologies and diverse viewing environments.

dashboard.intro: You can check the development status and specification overview of all WIM UI components.
dashboard.tokens_link: Token documentation is available in the Design Tokens section.

doc.baseListItem_a11y_desc: The root element uses appropriate list semantics. Interactive items receive keyboard focus and role attributes.
doc.baseListItem_anatomy_content_desc: Holds primary text, secondary text, icons, or any slotted children.
doc.baseListItem_anatomy_root_desc: The outermost container that defines layout, spacing, and hover state.
doc.baseListItem_best_practice_1: Avoid nesting list items more than two levels deep to maintain readability.
doc.baseListItem_best_practice_2: Ensure interactive items have a visible focus ring for keyboard accessibility.
doc.baseListItem_choice_alt_when: Use Paragraph for plain text content that does not need list-item structure.
doc.baseListItem_choice_matrix_desc: Comparing BaseListItem against other list and paragraph patterns.
doc.baseListItem_choice_use_when: Use for structured list items that may contain icons, actions, or supplemental content.
doc.baseListItem_content_guideline_1: Keep primary text concise — aim for a single line where possible.
doc.baseListItem_content_guideline_2: Use secondary text sparingly to provide supporting context.
doc.baseListItem_design_intent: BaseListItem provides a consistent visual and structural foundation for all list-based UI patterns.
doc.baseListItem_i18n_desc: All text content is provided by the parent consumer; the component itself has no hardcoded strings.
doc.baseListItem_keyboard_desc: When rendered as a button or link, supports Enter and Space activation and standard Tab navigation.
doc.baseListItem_motion_desc: Hover and focus transitions use a subtle background-color change for visual feedback.
doc.baseListItem_responsive_desc: The component adapts its padding on smaller viewports; icon and action widths remain fixed.
doc.baseListItem_scenario_1: A settings page where each row describes a configurable option.
doc.baseListItem_scenario_2: A form sidebar listing related resources or quick-link items.
doc.baseListItem_tokens_desc: Spacing, border-radius, and color tokens are used for padding, hover state, and divider styling.
doc.best_practice_footer: Focus on clarity and purpose in every section.
doc.fieldTemplate_a11y_desc: Connects the label to the input via id, and the error message via aria-describedby.
doc.fieldTemplate_anatomy_content_desc: The actual component slot (Input, Select, etc.).
doc.fieldTemplate_anatomy_error_desc: Validation feedback text displayed below the field.
doc.fieldTemplate_anatomy_label_desc: The name of the field displayed above or beside the input.
doc.fieldTemplate_anatomy_required_desc: An asterisk or (required) text appended to the label.
doc.fieldTemplate_best_practice_1: Use horizontal layout for dense desktop forms.
doc.fieldTemplate_best_practice_2: Clearly highlight required fields to reduce user errors.
doc.fieldTemplate_choice_alt_when: Use InputBase directly for internal controls or dense UIs where a label is not required.
doc.fieldTemplate_choice_matrix_desc: Comparing FieldTemplate against using InputBase directly.
doc.fieldTemplate_choice_use_when: Use for virtually all user-facing form fields that require a label, error message, or required indicator.
doc.fieldTemplate_content_guideline_2: Error messages should be specific and actionable.
doc.fieldTemplate_design_intent: Standardizes label and error presentation to ensure consistent form layout across the product.
doc.fieldTemplate_i18n_desc: Label and error text are provided by the consumer; the component itself has no hardcoded strings.
doc.fieldTemplate_keyboard_desc: Focus management follows the wrapped input component behavior.
doc.fieldTemplate_motion_desc: Error messages fade in to draw attention without being disruptive.
doc.fieldTemplate_scenario_2: A settings page with vertical or horizontal field layouts.
doc.inputBase_a11y_desc: Ensures interactive icons have correct roles and labels.
doc.inputBase_anatomy_content_desc: Space for the actual input value or selected value.
doc.inputBase_anatomy_root_desc: The main wrapper that defines the border and background.
doc.inputBase_best_practice_1: Use semantic colors for validation states.
doc.inputBase_best_practice_2: Always provide a visible label externally (via FieldTemplate).
doc.inputBase_choice_alt_when: Use FieldTemplate when you need to wrap an existing input component with a label or error message.
doc.inputBase_choice_use_when: Use when building a custom input field (e.g., Search, Select) that needs uniform border and icon styling.
doc.inputBase_content_guideline_1: Keep icon usage minimal to avoid visual clutter.
doc.inputBase_content_guideline_2: Ensure the focus ring is clearly visible.
doc.inputBase_design_intent: InputBase provides a consistent UI shell for text-based inputs, managing icon placement, clear button, and semantic state styling.
doc.inputBase_i18n_desc: All visible text is provided by the consumer; InputBase itself has no hardcoded strings.
doc.inputBase_keyboard_desc: Delegates keyboard behavior to the wrapped native input element.
doc.inputBase_motion_desc: Subtle transitions on border color and clear button visibility.
doc.inputBase_responsive_desc: The container scales with its parent; icon widths remain fixed.
doc.inputBase_scenario_1: A custom search box with an action icon.
doc.inputBase_scenario_2: A complex input wrapper for specialized data entry.
doc.inputBase_tokens_desc: Border, background, and spacing tokens control the visual container styling.

dropzone.description: Drag and drop files here, or click to select

guide.action_std_concept_desc: To ensure a consistent user experience, all buttons and interactive elements must follow the same size scale (sm, md, lg) and handle loading states predictably.
guide.action_std_desc: Unified sizing and loading states across all action-oriented components.
guide.action_std_note_desc: Legacy components will be progressively replaced with the new standardized versions above.
guide.action_std_rule_1_desc: All buttons must use the sm, md, lg scale defined by design tokens.
guide.action_std_rule_2_desc: Interactive elements performing async operations must display a spinner or skeleton.
guide.action_std_rule_3_desc: Icons maintain a constant 8px gap from text labels.
guide.action_std_rule_4_desc: All interactive elements must have a visible 2px outline when focused.
guide.numeric_std_concept_desc: While <input type="number"> seems ideal for numeric input, it has inconsistent behavior across browsers, especially around scientific notation "e" and locale-specific thousands separators. This document aims to standardize these behaviors.
guide.numeric_std_desc: Guidelines and technical considerations for consistent numeric input handling across browsers.
guide.numeric_std_exp_notation_desc: Note that large numbers may still be converted to scientific notation in some contexts.
guide.numeric_std_firefox_behavior: Standard handling but with visual quirks
guide.numeric_std_problem_1_desc: In numeric mode, browsers differ in how they handle invalid characters.
guide.numeric_std_problem_2_desc: The "e" character is often allowed but can cause unexpected behavior for users.
guide.numeric_std_problem_3_desc: Native spin buttons and padding vary significantly across rendering engines.
guide.numeric_std_problem_4_desc: Some screen readers treat numeric inputs as spin buttons, which may be inappropriate for certain data types.
guide.numeric_std_recommend_desc: For most use cases including IDs and formatted numbers, use type="text" with appropriate semantic hints.
guide.numeric_std_safari_behavior: Allows "e" and treats as scientific notation
guide.numeric_std_spin_button_warning: Consider hiding native spin buttons via CSS for a more consistent UI.
guide.numeric_std_usage_do_1: Use step and pattern attributes to guide user input.
guide.numeric_std_usage_dont_1: Do not rely on browser-native spin buttons for precise control.
guide.numeric_std_usage_dont_2: Avoid type="number" when hyphens or spaces are allowed.
guide.numeric_std_usage_dont_3: Do not assume a valid number is always returned on the "input" event across all browsers.
guide.numeric_std_usage_warning_desc: For fixed-format numbers like credit cards or IDs, use type="text" with inputmode="numeric".

language.support: Supports English, Japanese, and Portuguese locales

sampletextforscrollarea: Sample text for scroll area. Sample text for scroll area. Sample text for scroll area. Sample text for scroll area. Sample text for scroll area. Sample text for scroll area. Sample text for scroll area. Sample text for scroll area. Sample text for scroll area. Sample text for scroll area.

token.bp_cq_criterion: Component is placed within a layout where the container width is unknown until runtime
token.bp_cq_examples: General-purpose UI components that can be placed anywhere in a layout, such as QueryBuilder, BentoGrid
token.bp_cq_note: Container queries are supported in all modern browsers (Chrome 105+, Safari 16+, Firefox 110+).
token.bp_cq_usage_desc: Apply `container-type: inline-size` to the component root element. Then use `@container` rules to switch descendant element styles.
token.bp_cq_why_desc: For example, when using QueryBuilder in a main area with a sidebar, the viewport might be 1200px but the component's actual allocated width could be 600px. A media query (768px threshold) would never trigger and the layout would break. Container queries respond to the actual container width, so behavior in Storybook's viewport settings matches the real app.
token.bp_cq_why_title: Why Use Container Queries for UI Components
token.bp_desc: WIM UI supports responsive design with the following breakpoints as layout switching criteria.
token.bp_down_desc: Used to apply styles only at or below the specified breakpoint (max-width).
token.bp_mq_criterion: Component defines the page structure, or is assumed to span the full viewport
token.bp_mq_examples: AppShell (sidebar toggle), Dialog, BottomSheet (portal-rendered), global spacing scale, device capability detection (prefers-color-scheme, prefers-reduced-motion)
token.bp_mq_vs_cq_desc: WIM UI uses both media queries and container queries. The deciding criterion is: "Should this component respond to viewport width, or to the width of its container?"
token.bp_mq_vs_cq_title: When to Use Media Queries vs Container Queries
token.bp_scss_desc: These tokens are defined as the `$breakpoints` map variable in `src/tokens/_breakpoints.scss`, with mixins provided for easily writing media queries.
token.bp_sm_device: Large smartphones or small tablets in portrait
token.bp_up_desc: Used in mobile-first layouts to apply styles at or above the specified breakpoint (min-width).
token.color_checker_desc: Check the contrast ratio between background and text colors to verify accessibility compliance.
token.color_ct_desc: Colors for text and icons placed on semantic color (background) surfaces.
token.overview_desc: A list of design tokens that form the foundation of WIM UI style definitions. We recommend using these variables to maintain consistent design.
token.type_alias_desc: To apply consistent styles across components, the global tokens above are provided with aliases prefixed with `--wim-`. We recommend using these direct alias variables when developing and customizing WIM UI components.
token.type_desc1: Definitions for font sizes, weights, and line heights to achieve consistent typography. Font sizes use a modular scale with a ratio of 1.125 (Major Second).
token.type_desc2: The scale ratio setting is managed by the `$font-scale-ratio` variable in `src/tokens/_spacings.scss`. Changing this value updates the entire system font ratio at once.
token.type_ff_desc: Font specifications by language used across the application. Appropriate fonts are automatically applied based on the `lang` attribute of the html/body tag.
token.type_lh_jpan_loose:
Loose (1.8)
Generously spaced for Japanese body text, especially on mobile or long-form content.
token.type_lh_jpan_normal:
Normal (1.6)
WIM UI standard line height. Best balance of readability and information density.
token.type_lh_jpan_tight:
Tight (1.4)
For headings and labels where higher information density is desired.
token.type_lh_latn_loose:
Loose (1.6)
Generously spaced for Latin text. Ideal for readability in long-form content.
token.type_lh_latn_normal:
Normal (1.4)
Standard line height for most app UI elements. Balanced density.
token.type_text_ex_large:
Extra Large Text (20.25px)
Uses a ratio of 1.125^2 ($font-size-2).
token.type_text_ex_small:
Extra Small Text (12.64px)
Uses a ratio of 1.125^-2 ($font-size-minus-2).
token.type_text_large:
Large Text (18px)
Uses a ratio of 1.125^1 ($font-size-1).
token.type_text_small:
Small Text (14.22px)
Uses a ratio of 1.125^-1 ($font-size-minus-1).
